---
title: "Week 15 — Final case synthesis"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

What claim can we responsibly make?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

A cumulative case that walks through design, graph reading,
modeling, inference, limitation, and communication.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

In-class workshop on a partial case; students draft a written
conclusion.

### Friday — quiz / application

Final synthesis: short review or final application case.

## Primary source basis

Custom cumulative case. Light framing borrowed from the IMS
"applications" chapters (Ch 3 / 6 / 10 / 15 / 23 / 27) where the
narrative model is reusable.

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Fully custom case scenario tying together earlier weeks; no new
content topic.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Combine design, visualization, modeling, inference, and
      limitation into a coherent reading.
- [ ] Communicate a statistical conclusion clearly.
- [ ] Distinguish statistical evidence from practical / actionable
      evidence.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Final case: TBD
- Review packet: TBD
